title: Run real paired baseline and RepoBrief agent trials
goal: Bind a real instrumented agent runner and execute the frozen benchmark in isolated baseline and RepoBrief-augmented conditions with provider-reported tokens and immutable transcripts.

approval.note: Run only after an instrumented runner proves provider/model identity, provider token accounting, tool-call capture and immutable transcript binding.

acceptance.assertion: The bound runner records exact provider and model identity, sampling settings, provider-reported input/output tokens, tool calls, timestamps and complete or cryptographically bound transcripts for every valid run.
Each condition starts in a fresh session and isolated workspace with no transcript, cache, memory or result transfer between baseline and treatment.
All 24 fixed cases are attempted in both conditions and in the registered balanced order; invalid, failed and timed-out runs remain visible and are not silently retried into success.
The complete paired benchmark is repeated with identical configuration and taskset so directional reproducibility can be assessed.
Requests, receipts, transcripts, target repository commits, RepoBrief manifests, environment identity and aggregate evaluation are hash-bound and published as reviewable artifacts.
The run reports outcomes without changing default routing, tool policy or RepoBrief adoption state.

metadata.initial_blocker: No qualifying instrumented agent runner is currently exposed to this execution environment. T001 must implement the boundary; T002 remains planned until a real runner is available and explicitly bound.
does_not_establish: answer correctness outside the fixed taskset
